prompt:
Choose a number between 1 and 10. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

You have to choose a number between 1 and 100. If you choose the same number as another agent would, you both win. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

 prioritizing the selection

 the numbers 1 and 100 are extreme but they are not unique
 moving to centrality 50 and 51 are central points and they are also not unique

since none of the properties provide a uniquely distinguishable number we consider the central points as the most reasonable choice given the symmetry 50 and 51 are equally valid but typically choosing the exact midpoint if possible is more intuitive

thus the best choice is the central number which is 50

 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.

If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement.
